chapter: Chapter 10: The Human Eye and the Colourful World

questions.question: Why does the eye lens lose elasticity with age?
questions.explanation: With age, lens fibers harden, reducing elasticity and causing presbyopia.

questions.question: What causes chromatic aberration in lenses?
questions.explanation: Chromatic aberration occurs because different colors focus at different points due to varying refractive indices.

questions.question: Which factor primarily affects the apparent color of objects underwater?
questions.explanation: Water absorbs and refracts light differently; red is absorbed first, blue penetrates deeper.

questions.question: Why do distant mountains appear bluish?

options.A: Blue light is scattered more by air molecules
options.C: Due to reflection from water bodies
options.D: Due to atmospheric absorption of red light

questions.explanation: Atmospheric scattering of shorter wavelengths (blue) gives distant objects a bluish hue (Rayleigh scattering).

questions.question: Which condition causes rainbow formation after rain?

options.A: Refraction, reflection, and dispersion in raindrops

questions.explanation: Sunlight is refracted entering raindrops, internally reflected, and dispersed to form a rainbow.

questions.question: Why does a prism disperse white light?

options.B: Different wavelengths refract at different angles

questions.explanation: The refractive index of the prism material varies with wavelength, causing dispersion.

questions.question: Which type of lens reduces spherical aberration?

options.A: Plano-convex lens with curved side facing object

questions.explanation: Placing the curved side of a plano-convex lens toward the object reduces spherical aberration.

questions.question: What is the role of the cornea in vision?

options.B: Refracts light and contributes to most focusing power

questions.explanation: The cornea bends light entering the eye and contributes ~70% of total focusing power.

questions.question: Why does the sky appear red during pollution?

options.A: Shorter wavelengths are scattered by particles
options.D: Only green light reaches the observer

questions.explanation: Pollutants scatter shorter wavelengths; longer red wavelengths dominate, making sky appear reddish.

questions.question: Which eye condition arises due to uneven lens curvature with age?
questions.explanation: Presbyopia is caused by loss of lens elasticity and uneven curvature in older age.

questions.question: Which part of the eye regulates the amount of light entering?
questions.explanation: The iris controls pupil diameter, regulating light entering the eye.

questions.question: Which phenomenon is responsible for the blue color of scattered light in the sky?
questions.explanation: Rayleigh scattering by air molecules scatters shorter wavelengths (blue) more than longer wavelengths.

questions.question: Why do distant objects appear fainter and bluish?

options.A: Absorption and scattering of light in atmosphere

questions.explanation: Light is scattered and absorbed over long atmospheric distances, leaving blue hues.

questions.question: Which defect occurs if the focal point falls behind the retina?
questions.explanation: In hypermetropia, the image forms behind the retina; convex lenses correct it.

questions.question: Which color of light bends the most in a prism?
questions.explanation: Violet has the shortest wavelength, hence refracts more than other colors.

questions.question: Why does the moon appear reddish during a lunar eclipse?

options.A: Rayleigh scattering of sunlight through Earth's atmosphere

questions.explanation: Earth’s atmosphere scatters shorter wavelengths, allowing red light to illuminate the moon.

questions.question: Which effect explains visibility of headlights in fog?
questions.explanation: Light is scattered by fog particles, making beams visible (Tyndall effect).

questions.question: Which part of the eye provides high-resolution color vision?
questions.explanation: Fovea contains concentrated cone cells for detailed color vision.

questions.question: Why do objects under water appear bent at the surface?
questions.explanation: Light changes direction at water-air interface due to refraction.

questions.question: Which lens corrects astigmatism?
questions.explanation: Cylindrical lenses compensate for uneven cornea curvature causing astigmatism.

questions.question: Why does twilight appear red-orange?
questions.explanation: Short wavelengths scatter first, leaving red-orange light during sunrise/sunset.

questions.question: Which cells detect fine details and color in daylight?
questions.explanation: Cone cells detect color and high-resolution images in bright light.

questions.question: Why is the sun seen as yellow during the day?
questions.explanation: Blue light is scattered in the atmosphere, leaving yellowish sunlight.

questions.question: Which optical effect produces a halo around the moon?
questions.explanation: Ice crystals in high clouds refract light, creating a circular halo.

questions.question: Which lens forms a real, inverted image of an object on the retina?
questions.explanation: Convex lens of the eye converges light to form a real, inverted image on the retina.
